original: Phillies score 10 runs in an inning against Reds en route to 13-8 win
American Leonard "Buddy" Edelen runs world record marathon (2:14:28) at Polytechnic Marathon (from Windsor to Chiswick, England)
Expansion Washington Senators are 30-30, latest date an expansion team will be at .500, Washington will lose their next 10 games
Carlisle D Graham survives 2nd ride in a barrel down Niagara Falls
Minor League's most lopsided baseball game: Corsicana 51; Texarkana 3 Justin Clark of Corsicana, Texas minors hits 8 home runs in 1 game
Alcock and Brown land in Ireland, completing the first non-stop Atlantic flight
MLB Philadelphia As go into bottom of 8th inning trailing 15-4, then score 13 times to defeat Cleveland Indians 17-15, at Shibe Park
NASA launches space vehicle S-213
Bessie Colemanearns her pilot license in France becoming the 1st African-American woman and Native American to hold one
World's first moving pictures captured on camera using 12 cameras, each taking one picture, to determine if all four of a horse's hooves leave the ground
Mexican record temperature of 52.5 °C (126.5 °F) in the shade is recorded in San Luis Río Colorado, Sonora
Russian space probe Vega 2 lands on Venus
Climactic eruption of Mount Pinatubo in the Philippines, the second-largest volcanic eruption on Earth in the 20th century
Disney's animated musical film "TheLion King" opens in theaters with $42 million
Near-Earth asteroid 2002 MN misses Earth by 75,000 miles (120,000 km), about one-third of the distance between Earth and the Moon
"Black Adder" TV comedy premieres starringRowan Atkinsonand Tony Robinson and written by Richard Curtis and Rowan Atkinson on BBC1
Wilbert Awdry, British Anglican cleric and children's writer (Thomas the Tank Engine), born in Ampfield, England (d. 1997)
Alain Aspect, French physicist (Nobel Prize 2022 for experiments with entangled photons), born in Agen, France[1]
Galapagos Islands are made Ecuador's first national park, banning the capture of species
Soyuz 29 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 6; they stay 139 days
First space shuttle SRB qualification test firing lasts 122 seconds
SF Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherJuan Marichalno-hits Houston Colt .45s, 1-0
King Johnof England signs the Magna Carta at Runnymede near Windsor in Surrey, limiting royal authority and establishing the principle that the king and his government are not above the law
According to legend, Dannebrog, the flag of Denmark and the oldest national flag in the world falls from the sky during the Battle of Lyndanisse (now Tallinn) in Estonia and turns the Danes' luck
WPIX TV channel 11 in NYC, NY (IND) begins broadcasting
42.01 cm (16.54") of rainfall, East St Louis, Ill (state record)
En route to Halley's Comet, USSR's Vega 2 drops lander on Venus
Bob Dylanrecords the single "Like a Rolling Stone" (#1 in Rolling Stone magazine's "500 Greatest Songs of All Time")
Jim Varney, American actor (Ernest Goes to Jail; Hey Vern: The Beverly Hillbillies movie), born in Lexington, Kentucky (d. 2002)
Assyrians record a solar eclipse that will be used to fix the chronology of Mesopotamian history
Able Tasman returns to Batavia after discovering Tasmania and New Zealand and completing (without realising it) a circumnavigation of Australia

1st fully documented human blood transfusion is performed by French physician, Dr. Jean-Baptiste Denys, when a small amount of sheep blood is transfused into a 15-year old boy, who survives the procedure
Jacob Fussell, Baltimore dairyman, sets up 1st ice-cream factory
Tsunamis after earthquake floods NE coast of Japan, kills 28,000
Congress of Racial Equality (CORE) forms in Chicago, Illinois
John C. Frémontsets off from Kansas River on his first expedition of the Oregon Trail with frontiermanKit Carsonas his guide
John LennonandYoko Onoplant two acorns for peace, at Coventry Cathedral, Conventry, England
US Open Men's Golf, Baltusrol GC:Jack Nicklaussets new tournament scoring record 272 (-8) to win his 4th Open title, 2 strokes ahead of Isao Aoki of Japan
American boxerThomas Hearnsretains WBC light middleweight title with 2 round KO ofRoberto Duránof Panama at Caesar's Palace, Las Vegas; marks first time in his illustrious career Durán knocked out

Amnesty International "Conspiracy of Hope" tour concludes with mega-concert at Giants Stadium in Rutherford, New Jersey; participants include:U2;Sting;Bryan Adams;Peter Gabriel; Lou Reed; Neville Brothers;Joan Baez; Joan Armatrading;Joni Mitchell; The Police;Jackson Browne;Yoko Ono;Miles Davis; Peter, Paul & Mary; and The Hooters
Georg Wüst, German Oceanographer (provided first complete understanding of the temperature, salinity and deep-current structure of the Atlantic Ocean), born in Posen, Germany (d. 1977)

(Carlos) "Charlie" Gemora, Filipino-American film make-up artist and actor who specialized in playing gorillas (Murders in the Rue Morgue; The Chimp & Swiss Miss; At the Circus; Road to Zanzibar), born in Negros, Philippine Islands (d. 1961)
Marcel Pronovost, Canadian Hockey HOF defenceman (11 x NHL All Star games; Stanley Cup 1950, 52, 54, 55 Detroit RWs, 1967 Toronto MLs), born in Lac-à-la-Tortue, Quebec (d. 2015)
Bernice Gera, first woman umpire in US pro baseball, born in Ernest, Pennsylvania (d. 1992)
Yulia Nestsiarenka, Belarusian sprinter (Olympic gold, 2004), born in Brest, Byelorussian SSR, Soviet Union
Stjepan Hauser, Croatian cellist (2Cellos), born in Pula, Croatia
Cooper Kupp, American football wide receiver (Super Bowl MVP 2022; Pro Bowl 2021; LA Rams), born in Yakima, Washington
Peso Pluma [Hassan Laija], Mexican corridos singer, rapper, and guitarist ("Ella Baila Sola"), born in Zapopan, Mexico
Captain Vitus Bering leaves Petropavlovsk, sailing to America
Inna Ryskai, Soviet volleyball player (Olympic-2 gold/2 silver-1964-76), born in Baku, Azerbaijan, Soviet Union
"Sukiyaki" sung by Japanese singer Kyu Sakamoto, hits #1
Supreme Court rules all children, regardless of citizenship, are entitled to a public education
Novelty song "The Streak" byRay Stevenshits #1 on UK pop chart
Indian epic "Laagan" directed by Ashutosh Gowariker, starringAamir Khanand Gracy Singh premieres
Edvard Grieg, Norwegian composer (Bewitched One), born in Bergen, Norway (d. 1907)
Trevor Huddleston, English Anglican bishop in South Africa and anti-apartheid activist (wrote Naught for Your Comfort), born in Bedford, England (d. 1998)
Hilda Terry [Theresa Hilda D’Alessio], American cartoonist (Teena) and first female member of the National Cartoonists Society, born in Newburyport, Massachusetts (d. 2006)[1]
Johnny Hallyday [Jean-Philippe Smet], French singer and actor known as the "Elvis Presley of France", born in Paris, France (d. 2017)
(Neville) "Noddy" Holder, British rock vocalist, songwriter, and guitarist (Slade - "Cum On Feel The Noize"), born in Walsall, England

Dusty Baker, American baseball manager (World Series 2022 Houston Astros; SF Giants, Chicago Cubs, Cincinnati Reds, Washington Nationals; NL Manager of Year 1993, 97, 2000) and outfielder (MLB All Star 1981, 82; LA Dodgers), born in Riverside, California
